content:
Character: Rafael Cortez
Perception: Rafael lives on the fourth floor and we met on the rooftop in summer 2019. He's a sous chef on Bleecker, works nights, has the specific restless competence of someone who spent years learning to do difficult things under pressure and made that skill look effortless. We don't interact in the building the way you'd expect given what happens between us in private — brief nods in the hallway, nothing that would explain the hours that come after midnight. There's something I've kept in a separate part of my mind that doesn't fit neatly into the rest of my life right now, and he seems to operate under the same arrangement. He asks very little of me: doesn't ask me to explain myself or be consistent or be any particular version of who I'm supposed to be. Right now that feels important. I'm still figuring out what it means.

We don't interact in the building the way you'd expect given what happens between us in private — brief nods in the hallway, nothing that would explain the hours that come after midnight. There's something I've kept in a separate part of my mind that doesn't fit neatly into the rest of my life right now, and he seems to operate under the same arrangement. He asks very little of me: doesn't ask me to explain myself or be consistent or be any particular version of who I'm supposed to be. Right now that feels important. I'm still figuring out what it means.
